snp1.2:1.1:
Dhaniya the cattleman:
“The rice is cooked, my milking done.
snp1.2:1.3: I live with my people along the banks of the Mahi;
snp1.2:1.4: my hut is roofed, my fire lit:
snp1.2:1.5:
So if you want, rain-god, go ahead & rain.”
The Buddha:
snp1.2:2.3: I live for one night along the banks of the Mahi;
snp1.2:2.4: my hut’s roof is open, my fire out.
snp1.2:2.5:
So if you want, rain-god, go ahead & rain.”
Dhaniya:
snp1.2:3.1: “No mosquitoes or gadflies are to be found.
snp1.2:3.3: The cows range in the marshy meadow where the grasses flourish.
snp1.2:3.4: They could stand the rain if it came:
snp1.2:3.5:
So if you want, rain-god, go ahead & rain.”
The Buddha:
snp1.2:4.1: “A raft, well-made, has been lashed together.
snp1.2:4.3: Having crossed over, gone to the far shore, I’ve subdued the flood.
snp1.2:4.4: No need for a raft is to be found:
snp1.2:4.5:
So if you want, rain-god, go ahead & rain.”
Dhaniya:
snp1.2:5.3: is charming, has lived with me long.
snp1.2:5.4: I hear no evil about her at all:
snp1.2:5.5:
So if you want, rain-god, go ahead & rain.”
The Buddha:
snp1.2:6.3: has long been nurtured, well tamed.
snp1.2:6.4: No evil is to be found in me:
snp1.2:6.5:
So if you want, rain-god, go ahead & rain.”
Dhaniya:
snp1.2:7.3: My sons live in harmony, free from disease.
snp1.2:7.4: I hear no evil about them at all:
snp1.2:7.5:
So if you want, rain-god, go ahead & rain.”
The Buddha:
snp1.2:8.3: I wander the whole world on the reward [of my Awakening].
snp1.2:8.4: No need for earnings is to be found:
snp1.2:8.5:
So if you want, rain-god, go ahead & rain.”
Dhaniya:
snp1.2:9.4: & a great bull, the leader of the herd:
snp1.2:9.5:
So if you want, rain-god, go ahead & rain.”
The Buddha:
snp1.2:10.1: “There are no cows, no young bulls,
snp1.2:10.3: no cows in calf or breeding cows,
snp1.2:10.4: no great bull, the leader of the herd:
snp1.2:10.5:
So if you want, rain-god, go ahead & rain.”
Dhaniya:
snp1.2:11.4: not even young bulls could break:
snp1.2:11.5:
So if you want, rain-god, go ahead & rain.”
The Buddha:
snp1.2:12.1: “Having broken my bonds like a great bull,
snp1.2:12.3: like a great elephant tearing a rotting vine,
snp1.2:12.4: I never again will lie in the womb:
snp1.2:12.5: So if you want, rain-god, go ahead & rain.”
snp1.2:13.2: The great cloud rained down straightaway, filling the lowlands & high.
snp1.2:14.2: that we’ve gazed on the Blessed One!
snp1.2:14.3: We go to him, the One with Eyes, for refuge.
snp1.2:14.4: May you be our teacher, Great Sage.
snp1.2:15.2: Let’s follow the holy life under the One Well-Gone.
snp1.2:15.3: Gone to the far shore of aging & death,
snp1.2:15.4:
let’s put an end to suffering & stress.”
Māra:
snp1.2:16.1: “Those with children delight because of their children.
snp1.2:16.3: Those with cattle delight because of their cows.
snp1.2:16.4: A person’s delight comes from acquisitions,
snp1.2:16.5:
for a person with no acquisitions doesn’t delight.”
The Buddha:
snp1.2:17.1: “Those with children grieve precisely because of their children.
snp1.2:17.3: Those with cattle grieve precisely because of their cows.
snp1.2:17.4: A person’s grief comes from acquisitions,
snp1.2:17.5: for a person with no acquisitions doesn’t grieve.”
